item.title: Personal Independence Payment Call for Evidence
item.content:encoded: I have said since day one that changes made to Personal Independence Payment (PIP) need to be made by the people it will affect.

item.title: Dr Allison Gardner MP convenes first Monkey Dust Taskforce
item.content:encoded: Dr Allison Gardner MP has (17 April) convened the inaugural meeting of her Monkey Dust Taskforce, bringing together a wide range of local partners to confront one of the most complex public health and community safety ch...

item.title: Social Media Ban Consultation
item.content:encoded: As a former teacher and lecturer, I have seen first hand how technology can be a powerful tool for learning and connection. However, as your MP and someone who has worked closely with our local schools I worry about the ...

item.title: LRRG launches call for submissions
item.content:encoded: As a member of the Labour Rural Research Group (LRRG), I am committed to ensuring that the voices of rural communities are heard. Far too often, rural communities have been overlooked by policies that fail to account for...

item.title: Personally Speaking - The Impact of Chronic UTIs
item.content:encoded: When someone asks why I wanted to become an MP, the second reason I give always surprises. First, I want to stand up for all my constituents and second, it’s because I suffer from chronic UTIs – Urinary Tract Infections....

item.title: Personally Speaking - A town that's on the up!
item.content:encoded: Longton has my heart, it’s my special Valentine. I love its past, its present, and its potential. Of course, I’m no lone admirer, there’s a whole army of Longton lovers out there, all working their socks off to raise the...

item.title: Personally Speaking – Advanced Ceramics are the Future
item.content:encoded: As a proud MP backing the Potteries, I know there’s a future for ceramics, and I am fighting for it. From fine china and figurines; toilets and tiles; bricks and pipes; wherever we go we can find a piece of North Staffor...

item.title: MP Welcomes School Minister to Stoke-on-Trent
item.content:encoded: A LONGTON infants’ school has been commended as a model of best practice for its dedication and commitment to the provision of special educational needs and disabilities.

item.title: £800,000 REVAMP FOR LONGTON MARKET AS MP’S CAMPAIGN PAYS OFF
item.content:encoded: An £800,000 refurbishment that will breathe new life into Longton’s historic indoor market has been secured by Labour MP Dr Allison Gardner following a sustained campaign for extra investment.

item.title: Dr Allison Gardner MP leads action to confront Monkey Dust crisis
item.content:encoded: Dr Allison Gardner MP is taking action to tackle the growing harm caused by Monkey Dust by convening a city-wide taskforce to drive a coordinated response across Stoke-on-Trent.

item.title: MP welcomes banking hub as Lloyds announces closure
item.content:encoded: Stoke-on-Trent South MP, Dr Allison Gardner, today (WED) welcomed the announcement that a banking hub will be set up in Longton following the "regrettable" decision by Lloyds to shut their local branch.

item.title: Labour Protects Women and Girls from AI Deep Fake Images
item.content:encoded: I am deeply concerned by the failure of X, under Elon Musk's ownership, to prevent its AI tools being used to create sexualised and non-consensual images - particularly of women and children. This is a serious safeguardi...

item.title: Stoke-on-Trent South MP Welcomes Road Safety Strategy.
item.content:encoded: Stoke-on-Trent South MP Welcomes First Road Safety Strategy in Over a Decade

item.title: MP Fights for National Advanced Ceramics Hub
item.content:encoded: Dr. Allison Gardner MP Fights for North Staffordshire to Become National Advanced Ceramics Hub

item.title: Allison Gardner Announces Winners of Christmas Poem Competition
item.content:encoded: Dr. Allison Gardner, MP for Stoke-on-Trent South, today announced the winners of her annual Christmas Poetry Competition, celebrating the writing talent and festive spirit of her youngest constituents across the local ar...

item.title: MP Attends Key Ministerial Meeting on Advanced Ceramics
item.content:encoded: Allison Gardner MP met with the Minister for Defence Procurement, Luke Pollard MP, to discuss the critical role of North Staffordshire’s advanced ceramics industry in strengthening the UK’s defence supply chain.

item.title: Solar Farm Plan Raises Fears Over Threat to Wildlife
item.content:encoded: An area of North Staffordshire Green Belt the size of 130 football pitches could be turned into a giant solar farm raising concerns about the loss of prime agricultural land and the threat to wildlife.

item.title: MP Welcomes Police and Crime Commissioner Announcement.
item.content:encoded: Dr Allison Gardner MP Welcomes Police and Crime Commissioner Announcement but Demands Clarity on Fire Service Future

item.title: Dr Allison Gardner MP champions Longton’s revival in Parliament
item.content:encoded: Dr Allison Gardner MP has hailed Longton as “a town on the up” in a passionate speech to Parliament, celebrating the community spirit, creativity and hard work driving its revival.

item.title: MP Allison Gardner Attends Discussion to Fast-Track AI in the NHS
item.content:encoded: MP Allison Gardner Attends Key Panel Discussion to Fast-Track AI and New Technologies in the NHS

item.title: Allison Gardner Successfully Highlights Gambling Harms
item.content:encoded: Stoke-on-Trent South MP, Allison Gardner, Successfully Highlights Gambling Harms and Ensures Cross-Departmental Rehabilitation Support.

item.title: Dr. Allison Gardner MP Brings Vital SEND Support to Meir Park
item.content:encoded: Dr. Allison Gardner, Stoke-on-Trent South MP, Brings Vital SEND Support to Meir Park Community Hall

item.title: Allison Gardner Joins ADHD 360
item.content:encoded: Allison Gardner Joins ADHD 360 to Explore Future of ADHD Diagnosis, Highlighting Post-Menopause Challenges and the Role of AI

item.title: Wedgwood Temporary Production Suspension
item.content:encoded: Wedgwood ceramics are world renowned for their beautiful and rich history originating here in Staffordshire. I am proud that this piece of Staffordshire’s heritage is in my constituency, and I will fight hard to ensure t...

item.title: Fixing Britain’s Buses
item.content:encoded: As your MP for Stoke-on-Trent South, I know just how important local bus services are for so many people in our community, from getting to work, school, or appointments, to staying connected with friends and family. But ...

item.title: Supporting Dementia Action Week in Stoke-on-Trent South
item.content:encoded: This Dementia Action Week, taking place from 19–25 May 2025, I want to shine a light on one of the most pressing health and social challenges facing families in Stoke-on-Trent South — and one that I am determined to act ...

item.title: Let’s Get Number Confident This National Numeracy Day
item.content:encoded: Let’s Get Number Confident This National Numeracy Day – Wednesday 21st May!

item.title: Backing Our Locals Help Me to Find the Best Pub in Stoke-on-Trent
item.content:encoded: Pubs are more than just places to grab a drink, they're the heart of our communities. Whether it’s a birthday celebration, a quiz night, a quiet catch-up with friends, or a warm meal on a cold day, our pubs bring people ...

item.title: Listening to Draycott: A Village with Heart and Urgent Priorities
item.content:encoded: This weekend, I had the pleasure of visiting the lovely village of Draycott, a vibrant community with so much to be proud of, but also some pressing concerns that need to be addressed. I was warmly welcomed by Bev, one o...

item.title: A Brilliant Day at the Longton Pig Walk and Carnival
item.content:encoded: What a fantastic day we had at this year’s Longton Pig Walk and Carnival! I was incredibly proud to lead the Pig Parade alongside the irrepressible Lord Mayor, Lynn Sharpe. It was such a joyful, colourful celebration of ...

item.title: The Urgent Need to Fix Wheelchair Delays
item.content:encoded: I recently called for urgent action to address the unacceptable delays faced by individuals who rely on NHS-provided wheelchairs. Some of my constituents, along with residents in neighbouring parts of Staffordshire, are ...

item.title: Taking Our Vision to Number 10
item.content:encoded: Last week, I visited Number 10 as part of the Stoke-on-Trent delegation, alongside MPs, council representatives, and local stakeholders.

item.title: Pressure to Urge NatWest to Rethink Closure of Longton Branch.
item.content:encoded: As the Labour MP for Stoke-on-Trent South, I have recently been made aware of NatWest’s decision to close its Longton branch in June, and I am urging the bank to reconsider this move. For many in our community, this clos...

item.title: NHS Ten-Year Plan: A Vision for a Stronger Future
item.content:encoded: There is no doubt that the National Health Service (NHS) is the backbone of our well-being and quality of life. But it's no secret that the NHS is under immense strain. Now, more than ever, we need bold action to ensure ...

item.title: Fight to Save Hospital ‘Hub’
item.content:encoded: A campaign is underway to secure the future of a new, all-purpose hospital ‘hub’ planned for Longton.

item.title: Renters' Rights
item.content:encoded: Labour’s announcement about a new Renters Rights Bill will ensure that renters in the UK will have more secure rights when renting their property, it will provide stability to tenancies and help people feel secure in the...

item.title: Labour’s Plan to Fix Britain’s Roads and Support Drivers
item.content:encoded: Potholes, and the safety of our roads is a big concern of constituents across Stoke-on-Trent South. I hear from many of you that damage has been done to your cars because of the poor quality of roads.

item.title: A Path to Secure Homes for All
item.content:encoded: Addressing Britain’s Housing Crisis

item.title: Breaking Down Barriers, Building Opportunities for Every Child
item.content:encoded: Labour is taking decisive action to restore the confidence of families who have long been let down by the system for children with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND).

item.title: How Labour Are Supporting Small Businesses.
item.content:encoded: Small Business Saturday is a fantastic opportunity to celebrate the vital role of small businesses in driving our local economies and strengthening communities. Many of you have asked what Labour will do to support small...

item.title: £20 Million Boost for Buses in Stoke-on-Trent and Staffordshire
item.content:encoded: As you may have seen via my social media page, there is some exciting news for public transport users in Stoke-on-Trent and Staffordshire. A new £20 million investment has been secured to regenerate our local bus service...
